title: Billing Tiers Configuration
content:
This file defines the various billing tiers for the DocMed application, including their pricing, feature limits, and Stripe integration details.

## Key Exports and Functions:

*   **`TierId`**: A union type representing the possible IDs for billing tiers (`free`, `basic`, `pro`, `enterprise`, `contact`).
*   **`BillingCycle`**: A union type representing the billing cycle (`monthly`, `yearly`).
*   **`YEARLY_DISCOUNT`**: A constant representing the discount applied to yearly billing (0.1 or 10%).
*   **`TierConfig`**: An interface defining the structure of a single billing tier, including `id`, `name`, `priceMonthly`, `activePairLimit`, `monthlyInitLimit`, `stripePriceId` (for monthly and yearly), `features`, `highlighted`, and `trialDays`.
*   **`getEffectiveMonthlyPrice(tier: TierConfig, cycle: BillingCycle)`**: Calculates the effective monthly price for a given tier and billing cycle, taking the yearly discount into account.
*   **`getYearlyTotal(tier: TierConfig)`**: Calculates the total cost for a year for a given tier, applying the yearly discount.
*   **`getStripePriceIdForCycle(tier: TierConfig, cycle: BillingCycle)`**: Returns the appropriate Stripe price ID for a given tier and billing cycle.
*   **`TIERS`**: A record mapping `TierId` to `TierConfig`, defining all available billing tiers.
*   **`PAID_TIER_IDS`**: An array of `TierId`s that represent paid tiers.
*   **`WEBHOOK_SWITCH_COOLDOWN_MS`**: A constant representing the cooldown period in milliseconds for webhook switches (1 hour).
*   **`getTierById(id: string)`**: Retrieves a `TierConfig` object by its ID, defaulting to the `free` tier if not found.
*   **`getTierByPriceId(stripePriceId: string)`**: Retrieves a `TierConfig` object by its Stripe price ID, searching both monthly and yearly IDs. Defaults to the `free` tier if not found.

## Usage Example:

```typescript
import { TIERS, getEffectiveMonthlyPrice, BillingCycle } from './lib/billing/tiers';

const proTier = TIERS.pro;
const monthlyPrice = getEffectiveMonthlyPrice(proTier, 'monthly');
const yearlyPrice = getEffectiveMonthlyPrice(proTier, 'yearly');

console.log(`Pro tier monthly price: ${monthlyPrice}`);
console.log(`Pro tier effective yearly price per month: ${yearlyPrice}`);
```
